# Security Policy

## Supported versions

Support status is repository-specific. Until a repository publishes a version policy, report the affected repository, commit or release identifier, and a minimal non-sensitive reproduction through the approved private channel.

## Reporting a vulnerability

Do not open a public Issue for a suspected vulnerability. Use GitHub private vulnerability reporting when it has been approved and enabled for the repository, or contact `<SECURITY_CONTACT_CHANNEL>` through the approved private route.

Do not include passwords, API tokens, cookies, private keys, personal data, or production logs in a report. Maintainers will acknowledge, triage, and coordinate disclosure according to the approved incident process. Response targets and public disclosure policy are `TBD` until Security approves them.
